meaning: n.作戏,滑稽表演adj.滑稽的,可笑的
examples: The book read like a black comic burlesque.
Lyrics in the song about a burlesque stripteaser were slightly changed to pass film censorship.
At the Mousetrap they were still doing burlesque, even in '72.
Though it seems almost burlesque, for many Mexicans it is painfully close to historical truth.
At Kleine Nachtrevue, it took just one quick snippet of burlesque to get us hooked.
